title: external range-energy calibration requirements audit

requirements_decision_table.ingredient: HRD raw pulse selection and B-stack penetration depth
requirements_decision_table.evidence: Raw ROOT gate reproduces S00 selected B-stave pulse records exactly; depth order is B2/B4/B6/B8 hit depth.
requirements_decision_table.traditional_or_ml_role: Both methods can use even-readout charge/amplitude and penetration depth with run-held-out validation.
requirements_decision_table.needed_for_per_event_proton_energy: Keep as internal observable; not sufficient for absolute incident or stopping energy.

requirements_decision_table.traditional_or_ml_role: Defines the strongest raw-only closure target; leakage checks exclude odd readout from features.
requirements_decision_table.needed_for_per_event_proton_energy: Useful quality gate, but duplicate readout is not external truth.

requirements_decision_table.ingredient: External charge-proxy uncertainty from downstream stack
requirements_decision_table.evidence: S14b propagated P04b charge term gives combined nominal res68 0.246237 traditional and 0.188507 ML, both above 0.10.
requirements_decision_table.traditional_or_ml_role: Stress-tests whether charge closure survives an externalized HRD proxy.
requirements_decision_table.needed_for_per_event_proton_energy: Must be replaced or anchored by calibrated light-yield/energy-deposit response.

requirements_decision_table.ingredient: Material budget before and inside the HRD stack
requirements_decision_table.evidence: Prior S14d material scan changes the raw-only proxy envelope; traditional res68 span 0.017251773416181122 to 0.02877029809629374, ML span 0.02079760407409461 to 0.03222012616130544.
requirements_decision_table.traditional_or_ml_role: Geometry/material variants can be propagated, not learned absolutely from HRD pulses.
requirements_decision_table.needed_for_per_event_proton_energy: Surveyed thicknesses, dead layers, support material, target-to-stack path length, and uncertainties.
requirements_decision_table.status: requires external detector survey or validated model

requirements_decision_table.evidence: Raw data identify which stave fired last, but not the absolute front-face, center, active thickness, or inactive gap convention.
requirements_decision_table.traditional_or_ml_role: Sets PSTAR depth anchors and therefore the energy scale of both methods.
requirements_decision_table.needed_for_per_event_proton_energy: Coordinate convention tied to physical stave positions and active volumes.

requirements_decision_table.evidence: S14b uses a configured plastic-scintillator PSTAR table only as a monotonic depth-order lookup.
requirements_decision_table.traditional_or_ml_role: Maps depth anchors to nominal proton CSDA energies; ML inherits this target.
requirements_decision_table.needed_for_per_event_proton_energy: Material-specific stopping powers and validation for the actual scintillator/support mixture.

requirements_decision_table.evidence: No Birks constant, quenching curve, or ADC-to-light-yield calibration is present in the raw HRD ROOT.
requirements_decision_table.traditional_or_ml_role: Unmodeled response can make charge ranks look good while absolute energy is biased.
requirements_decision_table.needed_for_per_event_proton_energy: Bench calibration or validated Birks/quenching model with uncertainty propagation.

requirements_decision_table.evidence: The run condition is 190 MeV p on CD2, but HRD-only selected pulses do not label event-level proton, deuteron, fragment, or background species.
requirements_decision_table.traditional_or_ml_role: Neither method can convert a raw closure target into proton-only truth labels.
requirements_decision_table.needed_for_per_event_proton_energy: Independent PID, beamline tag, or validated stopping-depth truth sample.

requirements_decision_table.evidence: Depth-order violation is zero by construction for the raw proxy, but no external range telescope or MC truth validates true stopping depth.
requirements_decision_table.traditional_or_ml_role: A leakage-free model may still predict the proxy rather than physical stopping depth.
requirements_decision_table.needed_for_per_event_proton_energy: External range/stopping validation or simulation validated against calibration data.

requirements_decision_table.evidence: S14b reports no train/held-out run overlap, no event-key overlap, feature exclusion of run/event/odd readout, and shuffled-target ML res68 0.319485.
requirements_decision_table.traditional_or_ml_role: Required because raw-only closure is strong enough to make leakage plausible.
requirements_decision_table.needed_for_per_event_proton_energy: Keep these controls, then repeat them against external truth/calibration targets.

finding: The raw HRD reproduction gate matches S14b/S00 exactly at 640,737 selected B-stave pulses. The S14b run-held-out proxy closure remains strong for the internal duplicate-readout target (traditional res68 0.0212, ML res68 0.0250), but the propagated external charge-proxy uncertainty gives combined nominal range-energy proxy res68 0.2462 traditional and 0.1885 ML, both failing the 0.10 preflight threshold. Raw HRD data can constrain pulse selection, penetration ordering, duplicate-readout closure, and leakage controls; a per-event proton energy claim still requires external material budget, stave geometry/active-depth convention, Birks/quenching or light-yield calibration, proton/PID truth, and stopping-depth validation.
